---
title: "Lecture 5: HTML Lists & Images"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 2, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 5]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[HTML Lists & Images]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 2 | Unit I: HTML Forms & CSS Introduction<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Understand ordered, unordered, and definition lists
- Learn when to use each list type
- Insert and optimize images in web pages
- Implement responsive image techniques
:::

::: {.notes}
Lists are fundamental HTML structures used for navigation, content organization, and data presentation. Images make pages visually engaging but require optimization.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Types of Lists"}

[HTML List Types]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: columns
::: {.column width="33%"}
[**Unordered List**]{.teal-text}

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Use `<ul>`
- Bullet points
- No order
:::
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
[**Ordered List**]{.teal-text}

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Use `<ol>`
- Numbered
- Sequence matters
:::
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
[**Description List**]{.teal-text}

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Use `<dl>`
- Term + definition
- Pairs of items
:::
:::
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Unordered Lists"}

[Unordered Lists `<ul>`]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Best for items with no specific order:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2-5|6"}
<ul>
    <li>First item</li>
    <li>Second item</li>
    <li>Third item</li>
    <li>Fourth item</li>
</ul>
```

**Renders as:**
- First item
- Second item
- Third item
- Fourth item

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
Each `<li>` is one bullet point
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Ordered Lists"}

[Ordered Lists `<ol>`]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Use when sequence or ranking matters:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2-5|6"}
<ol>
    <li>First step</li>
    <li>Second step</li>
    <li>Third step</li>
    <li>Fourth step</li>
</ol>
```

**Renders as:**
1. First step
2. Second step
3. Third step
4. Fourth step

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
Automatically numbered by browser
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Nested Lists"}

[Nested Lists]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

You can put lists inside other lists:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2|3-5|6|7|8"}
<ul>
    <li>Frontend Skills
        <ul>
            <li>HTML</li>
            <li>CSS</li>
        </ul>
    </li>
    <li>Backend Skills</li>
</ul>
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Indentation shows hierarchy]{.teal-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Description Lists"}

[Definition/Description Lists]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

Perfect for term-definition pairs:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1|2-3|4-5|6"}
<dl>
    <dt>HTML</dt>
    <dd>HyperText Markup Language - structure</dd>
    <dt>CSS</dt>
    <dd>Cascading Style Sheets - styling</dd>
</dl>
```

::: {.body-text-m .topbr}
- `<dt>` = term
- `<dd>` = definition (indented)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="The Image Element"}

[Images in HTML]{.slide-title}

<hr>

The `<img>` tag embeds images in web pages:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1"}
<img src="logo.png" alt="Company Logo" width="200" height="150">
```

::: {.body-text-m .topbr}
**Key attributes:**
- `src`: Path to image file
- `alt`: Alternative text (for accessibility)
- `width`, `height`: Size in pixels
- `title`: Tooltip on hover
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Image Sources"}

[Image File Formats]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
::: {.fragment}
[**PNG**]{.teal-text} - Lossless, supports transparency, good for graphics
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**JPEG**]{.teal-text} - Lossy, smaller file size, best for photos
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**GIF**]{.teal-text} - Animated, limited colors, mostly for simple images
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**WebP**]{.teal-text} - Modern format, smaller files, better quality
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**SVG**]{.teal-text} - Vector format, scales infinitely, perfect for logos
:::
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Image Paths"}

[Image File Paths]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

**Relative paths** (recommended):

```html
<img src="images/photo.jpg" alt="Description">
<img src="../images/logo.png" alt="Logo">
<img src="./images/icon.png" alt="Icon">
```

**Absolute paths** (not recommended):

```html
<img src="https://example.com/images/photo.jpg" alt="Photo">
<img src="C:/Users/Name/Pictures/photo.jpg" alt="Photo">
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Use relative paths - they work when you move files!]{.magenta-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Responsive Images"}

[Responsive Images]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

Make images adapt to screen size:

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1"}
<img src="photo.jpg" alt="Description" 
     style="max-width: 100%; height: auto;">
```

Or using attributes:

```html
<img src="photo.jpg" alt="Description"
     width="100%" height="auto">
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Image shrinks on mobile, stays fixed on desktop]{.teal-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Image Best Practices"}

[Best Practices for Images]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
1. **Always use `alt` text** - Helps accessibility & SEO
2. **Optimize file size** - Use compression tools
3. **Choose right format** - JPEG for photos, PNG for graphics
4. **Use relative paths** - Makes sites portable
5. **Provide dimensions** - Prevents layout shift
6. **Responsive design** - Use width: 100% where appropriate
7. **Keep organized** - Store in `images/` folder
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Image Example"}

[Complete Image Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1-7"}
<figure>
    <img src="images/portrait.jpg" 
         alt="Portrait of John Doe"
         width="300" 
         height="400"
         style="max-width: 100%; height: auto;">
    <figcaption>John Doe - 2026</figcaption>
</figure>
```

::: {.fragment}
- `<figure>`: Container for image + caption
- `<figcaption>`: Image description
- Responsive sizing with max-width
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Resources"}

[Resources & References]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- [MDN: Lists](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/ul)
- [MDN: Images](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/img)
- [Image Optimization Guide](https://web.dev/image-optimization/)
- [Course content: Week 2](/content/week-02.qmd)
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 6 - HTML Hyperlinks & Tables
</div>
</div>
